title: Leaking Circuit Secrets: Gradient Leakage Attacks on Graph Neural Networks
authors: Rupesh Raj Karn, Johann Knechtel, Ozgur Sinanoglu
abstract: As graph neural networks (GNNs) become standard tools for critical tasks in circuit design and analysis, their security and privacy risks require careful attention. Here, we present the first comprehensive evaluation of gradient leakage attacks (GLAs) on GNNs in circuit-design and hardware-security tasks, a practical threat that has been largely overlooked. We assess state-of-the-art (SOTA) GNNs, including GraphSAGE, GCN, GIN, and GAT, trained on standard netlist benchmarks (ISCAS'85, EPFL, and TrustHub), for their fundamental vulnerability to GLAs. We find that GLAs can expose sensitive information, such as gate types and distinctive properties of hardware Trojans, which may assist adversaries in analyzing logic locking schemes or evading Trojan detection mechanisms. Our analysis shows that these risks are influenced by architectural features, with attention mechanisms (GAT) exacerbating leakage, while injective aggregation (GIN) provides comparatively stronger resilience. We further evaluate several SOTA defense techniques, including differential privacy, gradient clipping, secure aggregation, model compression with quantization, and adversarial training. We find that these techniques improve resilience only in specific settings and can also compromise model performance. Overall, our work provides key insights toward privacy-preserving GNNs and highlights the need for more robust and efficient defenses. We release our full methodology and artifacts.
title_zh: 泄露电路秘密：图神经网络的梯度泄露攻击
abstract_zh: 随着图神经网络（GNN）成为电路设计与分析中关键任务的标准工具，其安全性和隐私风险亟需关注。本文首次系统评估了梯度泄漏攻击（GLA）在电路设计与硬件安全任务中对GNN的威胁——这一实际风险此前长期被忽视。我们研究了包括GraphSAGE、GCN、GIN和GAT在内的最先进（SOTA）GNN模型，这些模型基于标准网表基准（ISCAS'85、EPFL和TrustHub）进行训练，以评估其面对GLA时的根本脆弱性。研究发现，GLA可能暴露敏感信息，例如门电路类型及硬件木马的独特性状，这或可帮助攻击者分析逻辑加密方案或规避木马检测机制。我们的分析表明，此类风险受架构特性影响：注意力机制（GAT）会加剧信息泄露，而注入式聚合（GIN）则展现出相对更强的抗性。我们进一步评估了多种SOTA防御技术，包括差分隐私、梯度裁剪、安全聚合、量化模型压缩及对抗训练。结果表明，这些技术仅在特定场景下提升抗性，且可能损害模型性能。总体而言，本研究为隐私保护型GNN提供了关键见解，并凸显了对更鲁棒、更高效防御方案的需求。我们已公开完整方法与相关工具。

title: Helpful or Harmful? Evaluating LLM-Assisted Vulnerability Patching via a Human Study
authors: Giulian Biolo, Michael Tezza, Yuanjun Gong, Fabio Massacci
abstract: Software vulnerability remediation is a cognitively demanding task that requires specialized security expertise often lacking in general developers. In the meantime, Large Language Models (LLMs) assisted tools show potential in vulnerability detection, location, and repair tasks. [Hypothesis:] While LLM-assistance is hypothesized to accelerate patching, it also risks introducing hallucinations or insecure code, leading to a higher likelihood of generating superficial repairs that bypass the standard functionality checks but fail the security validation. [Objective:] We aim to present an empirical experiment, unveiling the capability of LLM-assisted vulnerability patching compared to manual debugging on human participants in real-world scenarios. [Method:] We plan to conduct a controlled experiment using a Balanced Crossover design. For that, we have developed a WebApp for code execution and integrated hidden Ghost Tests to verify patch integrity beyond visible functional requirements. The experiment involves training and evaluation scenarios. The remediation speed, remediation efficacy for both standard functionality tests and security tests, and participant perception will be evaluated. [Pilot Study:] A pilot experiment with a small sample of participants has been conducted, providing insights for the following study.
title_zh: 有益还是有害？通过人类研究评估LLM辅助的漏洞修复
abstract_zh:
软件漏洞修复是一项认知要求极高的任务，需要专门的安全专业知识，而普通开发人员往往缺乏这类知识。与此同时，大型语言模型（LLM）辅助工具在漏洞检测、定位和修复任务中展现出潜力。  
【假设】尽管LLM辅助有望加速补丁开发，但也可能引入幻觉或不安全的代码，从而更易生成绕过标准功能检查但未能通过安全验证的肤浅修复方案。  
【目标】我们旨在通过实证实验，揭示在真实场景中，LLM辅助漏洞修补与人工调试相比的能力差异。  
【方法】我们计划采用平衡交叉设计进行控制实验。为此，我们开发了一个用于代码执行的Web应用程序，并集成了隐藏的幽灵测试，以验证补丁在可见功能需求之外的完整性。实验包含训练和评估场景，将评估修复速度、标准功能测试与安全测试的修复效果，以及参与者的感知。  
【试点研究】已开展小规模参与者的试点实验，为后续研究提供了洞察。

title: Improved Large Language Diffusion Models
authors: Shen Nie, Qiyang Min, Shaoxuan Xu, Zihao Huang, Yuxuan Song, Yong Shan, Yankai Lin, Wayne Xin Zhao, Chongxuan Li, Ji-Rong Wen
abstract: Modern large language models are predominantly trained with autoregressive factorization and causal attention. We present \emph{iLLaDA}, an 8B masked diffusion language model trained from scratch with fully bidirectional attention. iLLaDA keeps the masked diffusion objective throughout pre-training and supervised fine-tuning (SFT), scaling pre-training to 12T tokens and fine-tuning on a 25B-token instruction corpus for 12 epochs. We further use variable-length generation for efficiency and introduce confidence-based scoring for multiple-choice evaluation. Compared with LLaDA, iLLaDA improves broadly across general, mathematical, and code benchmarks; for example, iLLaDA-Base improves by 21.6 points on BBH and 14.9 points on ARC-Challenge, while iLLaDA-Instruct improves by 14.5 points on MATH and 16.5 points on HumanEval. Despite its non-autoregressive training, iLLaDA also remains competitive with Qwen2.5 7B on several benchmarks. These results show that fully bidirectional diffusion training from scratch is a competitive path toward strong language models. Model weights and codes: https://github.com/ML-GSAI/LLaDA.
title_zh: 改进的大型语言扩散模型
abstract_zh: 现代大型语言模型主要采用自回归分解和因果注意力进行训练。我们提出了\emph{iLLaDA}，一个从头开始训练的80亿参数掩码扩散语言模型，采用完全双向注意力机制。iLLaDA在预训练和监督微调（SFT）阶段均保持掩码扩散训练目标，预训练规模达到12万亿tokens，并在250亿tokens的指令语料上进行12个epoch的微调。我们进一步采用可变长度生成以提高效率，并引入基于置信度的评分用于多项选择评估。与LLaDA相比，iLLaDA在通用、数学和代码基准测试上全面改进；例如，iLLaDA-Base在BBH上提升21.6分，在ARC-Challenge上提升14.9分，而iLLaDA-Instruct在MATH上提升14.5分，在HumanEval上提升16.5分。尽管采用非自回归训练，iLLaDA在多项基准测试中仍能与Qwen2.5 7B竞争。这些结果表明，从头开始进行全双向扩散训练是通向强大语言模型的一条有竞争力的路径。模型权重和代码：https://github.com/ML-GSAI/LLaDA。

title: AI-Assisted Computational Reproducibility on the FABRIC Testbed
authors: Komal Thareja, Paul Ruth, Berent Aldikacti, Michael Zink
abstract: Computational reproducibility remains difficult despite being central to scientific research. In this paper, we show how the international FABRIC testbed, combined with large language model (LLM) coding assistants through LoomAI, can simplify reproducing published experiments across multiple domains. We reproduced three case studies on FABRIC, covering BBR-family congestion-control evaluations, LAMMPS molecular dynamics scaling benchmarks on a CPU-only MPI cluster, and stress protein homeostasis genomics pipelines. Rather than focusing only on matching numerical outputs, we evaluate whether the reproduced experiments support the same scientific conclusions as the original studies. The AI assistant was effective in setting up the environment, adapting code, and debugging, but struggled with the analysis stages that lacked clearly defined workflows, which required human guidance to establish execution order and data dependencies. Across the case studies, the AI-assisted workflow reduced reproduction effort by roughly 4--6 times. We conclude with practical recommendations for improving AI-assisted reproducibility on research testbeds.
title_zh: FABRIC试验平台上AI辅助的计算可重复性
abstract_zh: 尽管计算可重复性对科学研究至关重要，但实现起来仍然困难。本文展示了如何通过国际FABRIC试验平台，结合基于大语言模型（LLM）的编程助手LoomAI，简化跨多个领域已发表实验的复现过程。我们在FABRIC上复现了三个案例研究，涵盖BBR系列拥塞控制评估、基于纯CPU MPI集群的LAMMPS分子动力学扩展基准测试，以及应激蛋白稳态基因组学流程。与单纯匹配数值输出不同，我们评估复现实验是否支持与原始研究相同的科学结论。AI助手在环境搭建、代码适配和调试方面表现有效，但在缺乏明确定义工作流的分析阶段存在困难——这些阶段需要人工指导以建立执行顺序和数据依赖关系。在三个案例研究中，AI辅助工作流将复现工作量减少了约4至6倍。最后，我们提出改进研究试验台上AI辅助可重复性的实用建议。

title: Evolving Quantum Error-Correcting Encodings for Molecular Simulation
authors: Kenny Heitritter, James Brown, Tarini Hardikar
abstract: Useful quantum algorithms require many coupled discrete design choices. We study LLM-driven evolutionary program synthesis -- a language model edits a program, an external verifier scores the result, and high-scoring programs are retained and re-mutated -- as a tool for quantum-computing research. As a case study, we apply this loop to the Generalized Superfast Encoding (GSE), a fermion-to-qubit encoding whose prior molecular constructions reach code distance $3$. The search discovered interpretable constructor programs whose codes have \emph{exact} distance $5$ on the molecular instances tested, and distance $6$ on one $20$-mode instance, under strict stabilizer-coset semantics. To our knowledge these are the first GSE/superfast encodings beyond distance $3$ for dense molecular Hamiltonians. A second search, guided by verifier analysis of the first artifact, found a circulant constructor that reaches a five-qubits-per-mode floor on the tested $12$-, $14$-, $16$-, and $20$-mode instances, with certified dense-rule fallback at the failing $18$-mode case. As secondary resource descriptors, in a code-capacity \emph{memory} comparison at $p=10^{-3}$ the resulting encodings use $4.2$--$5.0\times$ fewer data qubits than a scoped per-mode Jordan--Wigner $+$ $[[25,1,5]]$ surface route and have $3.4$--$8.2\times$ lower logical-failure rates under finite-weight decoding tables with explicit truncation brackets; we claim no circuit-level fault-tolerance or Trotter-cost advantage. The search trajectory illustrates a general operating lesson: rewarding distance alone selects trivial dense graphs, whereas holding verified distance fixed and rewarding compression selects structured rules.
title_zh: 面向分子模拟的演化量子纠错编码
abstract_zh: 实用的量子算法需要许多耦合的离散设计选择。我们研究了由大语言模型驱动的进化程序合成——语言模型编辑程序，外部验证器对结果进行评分，高分程序被保留并再次变异——作为量子计算研究的一种工具。作为案例研究，我们将这一循环应用于广义超快编码（GSE），这是一种费米子到量子比特的编码，其先前的分子构造实现了代码距离$3$。搜索发现了可解释的构造器程序，其代码在测试的分子实例上具有严格稳定子陪集语义下的\emph{精确}距离$5$，并在一个$20$模实例上达到距离$6$。据我们所知，这是首个针对稠密分子哈密顿量实现距离$3$以上的GSE/超快编码。第二次搜索由首次输出的验证器分析指导，发现了一个循环构造器，在测试的$12$、$14$、$16$和$20$模实例上达到了每模五量子比特的最低水平，并在失败的$18$模实例上提供了认证的稠密规则后备方案。作为次要资源描述，在码容量\emph{记忆}比较中，当$p=10^{-3}$时，所得编码使用的数据量子比特比有范围的每模Jordan–Wigner$+$$[[25,1,5]]$表面路线少$4.2$至$5.0$倍，并且在具有明确截断区间的有限权值解码表下，逻辑故障率低$3.4$至$8.2$倍；我们并未声称电路级容错或Trotter步成本的任何优势。搜索轨迹展示了一条通用操作教训：仅奖励距离会筛选出平凡稠密图，而保持验证后的距离固定并奖励压缩则会筛选出结构化规则。

title: OPERA: Aligning Open-Ended Reasoning via Objective Perplexity-based Reinforcement Learning
authors: Wenxuan Jiang, Zining Fan, Zijian Zhang, Xuecheng Wu, Hongming Tan, Haoyang Dai, Xiaoyu Li, Xuezhi Cao, Ninghao Liu
abstract: Reinforcement Learning (RL) has enabled LLMs to excel in objective reasoning tasks such as mathematics and code generation. However, applying RL to open-ended tasks, such as creative writing, remains challenging because LLM-as-a-judge reward models often exhibit stylistic biases and positional inconsistencies, leading to unstable supervision. To address this, we propose OPERA (Objective Perplexity-based Reflective Alignment), which replaces unreliable external judges with intrinsic rewards derived from perplexity dynamics. Specifically, we derive an intrinsic reward signal from perplexity dynamics, quantifying uncertainty reduction at critical reflective states. During the cold-start phase, we introduce a data synthesis method that leverages carefully designed guiding words to generate diverse reasoning traces, along with perplexity-prioritized rollouts that utilize internal log-probabilities to identify logically consistent reasoning branches. This pipeline yields a large-scale dataset comprising 20,000 high-quality reasoning trajectories. Empirical evaluations consistently demonstrate the scalability and efficacy of our approach in alignment for open-ended tasks. Implementing OPERA on Qwen3-8B establishes a new state-of-the-art among open-source models, achieving parity with or surpassing proprietary models like Gemini2.5 and MiniMax-M2.5 in some open-ended tasks. The code is available at https://github.com/pangpang-xuan/OPERA.
title_zh: OPERA：基于目标困惑度的强化学习对齐开放式推理
abstract_zh: 强化学习（RL）已使大型语言模型在数学和代码生成等客观推理任务中表现出色。然而，将强化学习应用于创意写作等开放式任务仍面临挑战，因为以LLM作为评判者的奖励模型常表现出风格偏差和位置不一致性，导致监督不稳定。为解决此问题，我们提出OPERA（基于客观困惑度的反思性对齐方法），该方法用源自困惑度动态的内在奖励替代不可靠的外部评判者。具体而言，我们从困惑度动态中推导出内在奖励信号，用于量化关键反思状态下的不确定性降低。在冷启动阶段，我们引入了一种数据合成方法，通过精心设计的引导词生成多样化的推理轨迹，并利用基于困惑度优先的展开策略，借助内部对数概率识别逻辑一致的推理分支。该流程生成了一个包含20,000条高质量推理轨迹的大规模数据集。实证评估一致证明了该方法在开放式任务对齐中的可扩展性和有效性。在Qwen3-8B上实施OPERA，使其在开源模型中达到了新的最先进水平，在部分开放式任务中达到甚至超越Gemini2.5和MiniMax-M2.5等专有模型。代码已公开于https://github.com/pangpang-xuan/OPERA。

title: IntentTester: Intent-Driven Multi-agent Framework for Cross-Library Test Migration
authors: Yi Gao, Ziyuan Zhang, Xing Hu, Xiaohu Yang, Xin Xia
abstract: Unit tests capture both functional checks and domain-specific knowledge, but this knowledge remains locked within individual projects and is rarely reused across libraries with overlapping functionality. Existing migration techniques based on structural code mappings (e.g., API signatures) often break down under divergent designs or cross-language settings, resulting in non-executable migrated tests. In this paper, we present IntentTester, a multi-agent framework for intent-driven test reuse. Instead of translating raw code, IntentTester abstracts tests into a language-agnostic Test Description Language (TDL), aligns them with semantically related entities and dependencies in a repository graph, and synthesizes executable tests through LLM-guided reasoning and iterative validation. This design enables cross-library and cross-language migration without manual intervention, producing migrated tests that existing structure-mapping approaches cannot achieve. We evaluate IntentTester on nine open-source projects across three domains (JSON, HTML, and Time) and two languages (Java and Python). IntentTester generates 2,776 syntactically correct tests with 85\% correctness; in comparison, the two baselines achieve 51\% and 43\%. Among them, 2,410 tests executed successfully, yielding a 74\% effectiveness rate. Beyond higher success rates, IntentTester also surfaced previously unknown defects including stack overflows, null dereferences, and parsing inconsistencies, several of which have been acknowledged or patched by maintainers. Our results show that intent-driven migration shifts the focus from code mappings to semantic alignment, allowing practical cross-library and cross-language test reuse while improving test quality and exposing implementation flaws.
title_zh: IntentTester：面向跨库测试迁移的意向驱动多智能体框架
abstract_zh: 单元测试既捕获功能检查，也包含领域特定知识，但这些知识仍局限于单个项目内部，很少能在功能重叠的库之间复用。现有的基于结构代码映射（如API签名）的迁移技术，在面临设计差异或跨语言场景时常常失效，导致迁移后的测试无法执行。在本文中，我们提出IntentTester——一个面向意图驱动测试复用的多智能体框架。与直接翻译原始代码不同，IntentTester将测试抽象为语言无关的测试描述语言（TDL），使其与仓库图中的语义相关实体及依赖关系对齐，并通过大语言模型引导的推理和迭代验证合成可执行测试。这种设计无需人工干预即可实现跨库、跨语言迁移，生成现有结构映射方法无法实现的迁移测试。我们在三个领域（JSON、HTML、Time）和两种语言（Java和Python）的九个开源项目上评估了IntentTester。IntentTester生成了2,776个语法正确的测试，正确率达85%；而两个基线的正确率分别为51%和43%。其中2,410个测试成功执行，有效率达74%。除了更高的成功率，IntentTester还发现了此前未知的缺陷，包括栈溢出、空指针引用和解析不一致等问题，其中多个已被维护者确认或修复。我们的结果表明，意图驱动迁移将重点从代码映射转向语义对齐，使跨库、跨语言的测试复用成为现实，同时提升了测试质量并暴露了实现缺陷。

title: Evaluating LLMs on Real-World Software Performance Optimization
authors: Ezgi Sarıkayak, Wenchao Gu, Hesham Ghonim, Chunyang Chen
abstract: Software performance optimization is a notoriously complex and manual task. Despite the growing use of Large Language Models (LLMs) for code refinement, we still lack benchmarks that capture how optimization actually happens in real-world codebases. Existing frameworks often oversimplify the problem by focusing on isolated functions or a single performance metric, missing the critical trade-offs between execution time and memory footprint, the inherent noise of the measurement environment, and the variability introduced by different input data and execution conditions. We address this by introducing SWE-Pro, a repository-level benchmark derived from 102 expert-written optimizations from open-source projects. Unlike previous benchmarks, SWE-Pro pairs each task with parameterized tests to evaluate runtime, peak memory, and Time-Weighted Memory Usage (TWMU) across varying input data and execution conditions under noise-aware measurement conditions. Our evaluation shows that current LLMs struggle significantly: runtime gains are negligible, and memory optimizations are nearly non-existent. This stands in sharp contrast to expert implementations, which achieve an aggregate speedup of 15.5x and peak memory reduction of 171.3x over benchmark tasks. Expert-written improvements are observed in 91.2% of tasks for runtime and 65.7% for peak memory. Our findings expose a substantial gap between current LLM capabilities and the demands of expert-level engineering.
title_zh: 评估大型语言模型在真实软件性能优化中的表现
abstract_zh: 软件性能优化是一项公认的复杂且需要手动处理的任务。尽管大型语言模型（LLM）在代码改进中的应用日益增多，但我们仍然缺乏能够反映真实代码库中优化实际过程的基准测试。现有框架往往将问题过度简化，只关注孤立的函数或单一的性能指标，忽略了执行时间与内存占用之间的关键权衡、测量环境固有的噪声，以及不同输入数据和执行条件带来的可变性。为此，我们引入了SWE-Pro，这是一个从开源项目中提取的、基于102个专家级优化案例的仓库级基准测试。与之前的基准测试不同，SWE-Pro为每个任务配对了参数化测试，用于评估在不同输入数据和执行条件下、在考虑噪声的测量环境中的运行时间、峰值内存以及时间加权内存使用量（TWMU）。我们的评估显示，当前LLM的表现严重不足：运行时间的提升微乎其微，而内存优化几乎不存在。这与专家实现的成果形成了鲜明对比——在基准测试任务上，专家实现实现了15.5倍的总加速比和171.3倍的峰值内存降低。在91.2%的任务中观察到专家对运行时间的改进，在65.7%的任务中观察到对峰值内存的改进。我们的发现暴露了当前LLM能力与专家级工程需求之间的巨大差距。

title: Weave of Formal Thought
authors: Alexandre Bouayad
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) attain remarkable surface fluency on code, yet they neither formally guarantee the syntactic validity of their output nor leverage the hierarchical structure defining the target language. While existing constrained-decoding frameworks address the former, they operate under rigid assumptions that preclude critical lexical mechanisms -- including context-sensitive lexing, maximal-munch tokenization, and keyword extraction -- and only approximate vocabulary masking, sacrificing completeness. For the latter, code LLMs typically inject grammatical structure via predetermined policies rather than learning which structural information to expose. In this work, we introduce Weave of Formal Thought (WoFT), a paradigm uniting rigorous syntactic validation with learned structural representations. First, we present a formal engine and constrained decoder that is sound and complete with respect to the full Tree-sitter specification. By augmenting generalized LR (GLR) parsing with a speculative-lexing construction that maintains concurrent lexer-state hypotheses synchronized with a GLR graph-structured stack, our decoder admits every subword token extending to a valid program prefix and rejects all others. Second, we present a latent-variable fine-tuning method training the language model to interleave non-terminal grammar symbols directly into generation. Utilizing the reweighted wake-sleep (RWS) algorithm to optimize the importance-weighted evidence lower bound (IW-ELBO) of the surface text, the model learns to selectively retain formal derivations as an adaptive structural scratchpad. For Python, fine-tuning StarCoder2-3B with our RWS objective reduces per-token cross-entropy by 14.3% relative to a text-only SFT baseline, demonstrating that discretionary latent syntax recovers critical structural information that flat autoregressive training discards.
title_zh: 形式思维的编织
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）在代码生成中展现出显著的表面流畅性，但它们既无法从形式上保证输出结果的句法有效性，也无法利用目标语言的层级结构特征。现有的约束解码框架虽能解决前者，却基于刚性假设运作，排除了关键词汇机制——包括上下文相关的词法分析、最大匹配分词和关键词提取——且仅近似实现词汇掩码，牺牲了完整性。针对后者，代码LLMs通常通过预定义策略注入语法结构，而非学习应暴露哪些结构信息。在本研究中，我们提出“形式思维编织”（WoFT）范式，将严格的句法验证与习得的结构表示相统一。首先，我们提出一个形式化引擎和约束解码器，该解码器相对于完整的Tree-sitter规范是正确且完备的。通过将广义LR（GLR）解析与推测性词法分析构造相结合，以维护与GLR图结构堆栈同步的并发词法器状态假设，我们的解码器接受所有可扩展为有效程序前缀的子词token，并拒绝其他所有token。其次，我们提出一种潜变量微调方法，训练语言模型直接将非终结符语法符号交织到生成过程中。利用重加权睡眠-觉醒（RWS）算法优化表层文本的重要性加权证据下界（IW-ELBO），模型学会选择性地保留形式推导作为适应性结构草稿。针对Python，使用我们的RWS目标微调StarCoder2-3B模型，相比纯文本SFT基线，每个token的交叉熵相对降低了14.3%，这表明离散潜语法能够恢复平面自回归训练所丢弃的关键结构信息。

title: CodeChat-Eval: Evaluating Large Language Models in Multi-Turn Code Refinement Dialogues
authors: Guoxiang, Guo, Kla Tantithamthavorn, Neelofar Neelofar, Yuanyuan Qi, Aldeida Aleti
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly used in software engineering to generate and refine code. In practice, developers often continue from an initial code generation request with follow-up refinement instructions, such as requests to improve style, restructure implementation, or change the execution strategy while preserving the intended behaviour. However, existing benchmarks generally omit this multi-turn code refinement dialogue setting and therefore cannot evaluate whether LLMs maintain functional correctness, i.e., whether the refined code still passes the test suite for the original task. To address this limitation, we introduce CodeChat-Eval, an evaluation framework that constructs evaluation sessions from multi-turn code refinement dialogues using a dynamic instruction selection algorithm. Our empirical study on open-weight and proprietary LLMs observes a statistically significant decrease ranging from 19.2% (GPT-5 Nano) to 69.2% (Llama 3.1 8B) in functional correctness over multi-turn refinement. The largest correctness drops are associated with logic-level refinements and additive change requests. These findings indicate that LLMs struggle to maintain functional correctness during multi-turn code refinement dialogues, and highlight the need for benchmarks that evaluate functionality-preserving refinement beyond single-turn generation.
title_zh: CodeChat-Eval：在多轮代码优化对话中评估大型语言模型
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）在软件工程中被越来越多地用于生成和优化代码。在实践中，开发者通常会在初始代码生成请求之后，继续提出后续的优化指令，例如要求改善代码风格、重构实现方式，或在保持预期行为的前提下更改执行策略。然而，现有基准测试通常忽略了这种多轮代码优化对话的设置，因此无法评估LLMs是否保持功能正确性，即优化后的代码是否仍能通过原始任务的测试套件。为了解决这一局限性，我们引入了CodeChat-Eval，这是一个评估框架，它通过动态指令选择算法从多轮代码优化对话中构建评估会话。我们对开放权重和专有LLMs的实证研究发现，在多轮优化过程中，功能正确性出现了统计学上显著的下降，范围从19.2%（GPT-5 Nano）到69.2%（Llama 3.1 8B）。最大的正确性下降与逻辑层面的优化和新增变更请求相关。这些发现表明，LLMs在多轮代码优化对话中难以维持功能正确性，并凸显了需要评估超出单轮生成的、保持功能的优化的基准测试。

title: Automated Detection of Configuration-Specific Security Vulnerabilities via Patch Analysis
authors: Felipe de Sant'Anna Paixão, Joanna C. S. Santos, Paulo Anselmo da Mota Silveira Neto, Daniel Sadoc Menasche, Gustavo Bittencourt Figueiredo, Eduardo Santana de Almeida
abstract: We study how security patches in highly configurable C/C++ systems map onto the space of compile-time variants. We formalize the Vulnerability Impact Condition (VIC) - a Boolean predicate over configuration options that denotes all variants that contained the original flaw - and introduce PatchLens, a purely static technique that recovers VICs by aligning AST-level patch hunks with source-level presence conditions and resolving file inclusion via lightweight build system analysis. Evaluating PatchLens on 1,192 Linux kernel, 289 FFmpeg, and 100 PHP patches, we compute precise, human-readable VICs without the need to compile any system variant. The resulting predicates are compact (avg. 1.84 variables for Linux, 3.23 for FFmpeg, 1.04 for PHP) and show that only a small fraction of vulnerabilities are system-wide, which carry higher CVSS scores; meanwhile, CVE texts almost never encode the required options ($\approx$ 1% average recall), motivating automated enrichment of CVE descriptions with VICs. PatchLens and the accompanying dataset enable immediate applications in CI (variant-aware triage and test selection), targeted sampling and fuzzing, and feature risk scoring, offering a scalable, explainable path to vulnerability assessment in highly configurable software.
title_zh: 通过补丁分析自动检测配置特定的安全漏洞
abstract_zh: 我们研究了高度可配置的 C/C++ 系统中安全补丁如何映射到编译时变体的空间。我们形式化了漏洞影响条件（Vulnerability Impact Condition, VIC）——一种基于配置选项的布尔谓词，用于描述所有包含原始缺陷的变体——并引入了 PatchLens，一种纯静态技术，通过将抽象语法树（AST）级别的补丁块与源代码级别的存在条件对齐，并结合轻量级构建系统分析来解析文件包含关系，从而恢复 VIC。我们在 1,192 个 Linux 内核补丁、289 个 FFmpeg 补丁和 100 个 PHP 补丁上评估了 PatchLens，在不编译任何系统变体的情况下，计算出了精确且人类可读的 VIC。生成的谓词非常紧凑（Linux 平均 1.84 个变量，FFmpeg 平均 3.23 个，PHP 平均 1.04 个），并且表明只有一小部分漏洞是系统范围的，这些漏洞通常具有更高的 CVSS 评分；与此同时，CVE 描述文本几乎从未编码所需的配置选项（平均召回率约为 1%），这推动了使用 VIC 自动丰富 CVE 描述的需求。PatchLens 及其附带的数据集可直接应用于持续集成（CI）中的变体感知优先级排序和测试选择、定向采样和模糊测试以及功能风险评分，为高度可配置软件中的漏洞评估提供了一条可扩展且可解释的路径。

title: Semantic Code Clone Detection: Are We There Yet?
authors: Zhiwei Xu, Weixian Deng, Xuyang Liu, Xiaolin Peng, Jiabao Gao, Tian Qiu, Hai Wan, Xibin Zhao
abstract: Code clone detection has been extensively studied for decades, and recent approaches have begun reporting remarkably high performance for semantic (Type-4) clones on benchmark datasets. However, it remains unclear whether these results reflect a genuine ability to capture semantic equivalence between programs, or simply an ability to exploit dataset-specific patterns. In this paper, we present the first systematic empirical study investigating the generalizability of state-of-the-art (SOTA) semantic code clone detectors beyond benchmark evaluation settings. Inspired by the inherent inclusion relationship among clone types, we propose a clone operator framework consisting of eight transformation operators derived from Type-2 and Type-3 clone variations. Using these operators, we construct distribution-shifted yet semantically equivalent Type-4 clone instances and evaluate 11 representative detectors spanning token-based, tree-based, and graph-based paradigms on the real-world BigCloneBench dataset. Our results reveal substantial performance degradation across all evaluated approaches, despite their strong benchmark performance. Further analyses show that existing detectors heavily rely on shortcut learning based on lexical and structural cues rather than robust semantic understanding. Our findings suggest that current SOTA semantic code clone detectors exhibit limited generalizability in real-world scenarios, highlighting important avenues for future research.
title_zh: 语义代码克隆检测：我们到了吗？
abstract_zh: 代码克隆检测已历经数十年的广泛研究，近年来，各类方法开始在基准数据集上针对语义（第4类）克隆展现出极高的性能。然而，这些结果究竟是真实反映了程序间语义等价性的捕捉能力，还是仅仅体现了对数据集特有模式的利用能力，仍不明确。本文首次系统性地开展实证研究，探讨当前最先进的语义代码克隆检测器在基准测试环境之外的泛化能力。受克隆类型间固有包含关系的启发，我们提出了一个包含八种转换操作符的克隆操作框架，这些操作符源自第2类和第3类克隆的变体。利用这些操作符，我们构建了分布偏移但语义等价的新型第4类克隆实例，并在真实世界数据集BigCloneBench上评估了11种代表性检测器（涵盖基于令牌、基于树和基于图的方法）。结果显示，尽管所有方法在基准测试中表现强劲，但在这些新实例上均出现了显著性能下降。进一步分析表明，现有检测器严重依赖基于词汇和结构线索的捷径学习，而非稳健的语义理解。我们的发现表明，当前最先进的语义代码克隆检测器在真实场景中泛化能力有限，这为未来研究指明了重要方向。

title: SafeGen: LLM-Driven Assertion Generation and Fault Criticality Evaluation for Functional Safety
authors: Xuanyi Tan, Arjun Chaudhuri, Rubin Parekhji, Krishnendu Chakrabarty
abstract: With advances in autonomous driving and electric vehicle technologies, functional safety has become a critical requirement in automotive chip design. Traditional simulation-based fault analysis is often overly conservative at the module level and fails to accurately reflect fault criticality. This paper presents SafeGen, an LLM-driven, formal-verification-assisted framework for functional-safety-oriented fault criticality assessment. SafeGen leverages large language models (LLMs) and a document-level Hyper Knowledge Graph (HyperKG) that incorporates Failure Modes, Effects, and Diagnostic Analysis (FMEDA) guidelines to extract verifiable specifications from design and safety documents and evaluate their relevance to overall system safety. The HyperKG is further enriched with register-transfer-level (RTL) information to guide the generation of Functional Safety Assertions (FSAs) that are both semantically grounded and design-aware. Each assertion is linked to its corresponding specification, enabling traceable reasoning throughout the assessment process. A gate-to-RTL fault-mapping mechanism supporting both stuck-at and bridging faults, combined with formal property verification (FPV), enables semantic-level fault criticality grading based on specification-linked assertion violations. A digital-physical co-simulation platform for a field-oriented control (FOC) system is developed to validate SafeGen. Experimental results demonstrate that SafeGen generates higher-quality assertions than existing LLM-based assertion generation frameworks while providing greater semantic interpretability in fault criticality assessment compared with traditional simulation-based approaches.
title_zh: SafeGen: 大语言模型驱动的断言生成与故障关键性评估（用于功能安全）
abstract_zh: 随着自动驾驶和电动汽车技术的进步，功能安全已成为汽车芯片设计中的关键需求。传统的基于仿真的故障分析在模块级别往往过于保守，无法准确反映故障关键性。本文提出SafeGen，一种由大型语言模型（LLM）驱动、形式化验证辅助的面向功能安全的故障关键性评估框架。SafeGen利用大型语言模型和包含故障模式、影响与诊断分析（FMEDA）指南的文档级超知识图谱（HyperKG），从设计文档和安全文档中提取可验证的规格说明，并评估其与整体系统安全的相关性。超知识图谱进一步融入寄存器传输级（RTL）信息，以指导生成既具有语义基础又具备设计感知能力的功能安全断言（FSA）。每条断言都链接到其对应的规格说明，从而在整个评估过程中实现可追溯的推理。一种支持固定故障和桥接故障的门级到RTL故障映射机制，结合形式化属性验证（FPV），能够根据与规格说明关联的断言违规情况实现语义级别的故障关键性分级。为验证SafeGen，开发了针对磁场定向控制（FOC）系统的数字物理协同仿真平台。实验结果表明，与现有基于LLM的断言生成框架相比，SafeGen能够生成更高质量的断言，同时在故障关键性评估方面相比传统基于仿真的方法具有更强的语义可解释性。

title: Croc: Training the Next Generation Chip Designers on Domain-Specific End-to-End Open Source Silicon
authors: Enrico Zelioli, Philippe Sauter, Thomas Benz, Hannah Pochert, Luisa Wüthrich, Beat Muheim, Frank K. Gürkaynak, Luca Benini
abstract: The demand for domain-specific systems-on-chip (SoCs) in artificial intelligence, robotics, and automotive systems is increasing the need for engineers with hands-on expertise on very-large-scale integration (VLSI) design from architecture specification to fabricated silicon. Yet, most VLSI courses rely on restrictively licensed electronic design automation tools and process design kits (PDKs), as well as closed-source hardware designs. We present an end-to-end open-source domain-specific SoC design and fabrication flow built around Croc, a highly customizable RISC-V platform. Built from open-source SystemVerilog intellectual property blocks and integrated with an end-to-end open-source design flow in a 130nm open PDK, Croc enables tapeout projects supporting multiple domain customization options: instruction-set extensions, accelerator co-processors, and peripherals. In our first open-source course experience using Croc, 65 students completed 33 projects, 30 of which produced manufacturable layouts. 18 designs were selected as tapeout candidates, and five were fabricated. A first baseline chip has already been successfully characterized in silicon, demonstrating microcontroller-class functionality and implementation metrics comparable to those of products with similar functional complexity completed with closed-source toolchains and PDKs.
title_zh: Croc：面向领域特定端到端开源芯片的下一代芯片设计师培养
abstract_zh: 人工智能、机器人和汽车系统中对领域专用系统级芯片（SoC）的需求日益增长，这促使工程师需要具备从架构规范到硅片制造的全流程超大规模集成电路（VLSI）设计实践技能。然而，大多数VLSI课程依赖受限制授权的电子设计自动化工具、工艺设计套件（PDK）以及闭源硬件设计。我们提出了一种基于Croc（高度可定制的RISC-V平台）的端到端开源领域专用SoC设计与制造流程。该流程采用开源SystemVerilog知识产权模块构建，并集成基于130纳米开源PDK的端到端开源设计工具链，支持面向多种领域定制选项的流片项目：指令集扩展、加速协处理器以及外设。在我们首次使用Croc的开源课程实践中，65名学生完成了33个项目，其中30个生成了可制造版图，18个设计被选为流片候选方案，5个完成流片。首个基线芯片已成功在硅片上完成特性验证，展现了微控制器级别的功能，其实现指标与采用闭源工具链和PDK、功能复杂度相当的产品不相上下。

title: LibEvoBench: Probing Temporal Knowledge Stratification in Code Generation Models
authors: Daniele Cipollone, Sergey Titov, Maliheh Izadi, Egor Bogomolov, Arie van Deursen
abstract: Large software projects often depend on older versions of libraries, even as APIs continue to evolve across releases. This creates a challenge for LLMs: they must maintain knowledge of multiple API versions, not merely the latest or most common one. However, current LLMs are trained on temporally mixed corpora and lack explicit mechanisms for such version-specific reasoning, leading to anachronistic errors - calling APIs as they exist in a different library version. To systematically evaluate this phenomenon, we introduce LibEvoBench, a multi-task benchmark spanning multiple versions of widely used Python libraries, along with a new metric, the Software Evolution Understanding Score (SEUS), to measure models' consistency when working with evolving APIs. Our results show that state-of-the-art models are largely version-oblivious: performance degrades for evolving APIs, while for stable APIs it remains the same across versions. Moreover, simply specifying the target version provides no benefit, while relevant documentation significantly boosts models' accuracy. These findings highlight a systematic limitation of current training paradigms and motivate new approaches for temporally grounded knowledge in code generation.
title_zh: LibEvoBench：探究代码生成模型中的时间知识分层
abstract_zh: 大型软件项目通常依赖旧版本的库，即使随着版本迭代，API（应用程序接口）也在不断演变。这给大语言模型（LLM）带来了挑战：它们必须掌握多个API版本的知识，而不仅仅是最新或最常用的版本。然而，当前的大语言模型基于时间混合的语料库进行训练，缺乏针对特定版本推理的明确机制，从而导致年代错乱的错误——即调用存在于不同库版本中的API。为了系统性地评估这一现象，我们提出了LibEvoBench，这是一个涵盖广泛使用的Python库多个版本的多任务基准测试，同时引入了一项新指标——软件演化理解评分（SEUS），用于衡量模型在处理演变中API时的一致性。我们的结果表明，最先进的模型在很大程度上对版本不敏感：对于演变的API，性能会下降；而对于稳定的API，其性能在不同版本间保持不变。此外，仅指定目标版本并无益处，而提供相关文档则能显著提升模型的准确性。这些发现凸显了当前训练范式的系统性局限，并推动了在代码生成中融入时间感知知识的新方法研究。

title: CrypFormBench: Benchmarking Formal Analysis Capability of Large Language Models for Cryptographic Schemes
authors: Zhaoxuan Li, Qionglu Zhang, Hengyuan Liu, Xiaoyan Gu, Xianhui Lu, Hongbo Liu, Bingzheng Wang, Haihui Fan, Ziming Zhao, Rui Zhang, Li Zhou
abstract: Manual formal analysis of cryptographic schemes is labor-intensive and requires substantial expertise. While model-checking tools (e.g., Scyther and Tamarin) and computational-security tools (e.g., CryptoVerif and EasyCrypt) improve the automation of security proofs, they still rely on experts to abstract schemes and write tool-specific formal descriptions. Large language models (LLMs) are a promising alternative, but their effectiveness in this domain remains unexplored due to the absence of standardized evaluation methodologies. To fill this gap, we introduce CrypFormBench (C.F.B for short), a comprehensive benchmark jointly covering symbolic and computational security to evaluate five core LLM capabilities: interpretation, generation, completion, transformation, and correction. It comprises 700 instances spanning 677 schemes, 7 mainstream formal verifier languages, and 160 security properties. The evaluation of 9 state-of-the-art LLMs reveals that most of them perform well on interpretation and completion, given their code-awareness advantages, but struggle with generation, transformation, and correction. Overall, their performance remains limited, with Claude-3.5 achieving the highest score at 48.7 out of 100. We further provide practical guidance, e.g., few-shot prompting, Pass@K sampling, and lightweight fine-tuning, to mitigate the executability bottleneck and improve tool-usable outputs. Taken together, our benchmark and analyses offer a grounded view of current progress and concrete directions toward reliable LLM-assisted formal cryptographic analysis.
title_zh: CrypFormBench：大型语言模型对密码方案的形式化分析能力基准测试
abstract_zh: 对密码方案进行人工形式化分析劳动密集且需要大量专业知识。尽管模型检测工具（如Scyther和Tamarin）与计算安全工具（如CryptoVerif和EasyCrypt）提高了安全证明的自动化程度，但它们仍需专家对方案进行抽象并编写特定于工具的形式化描述。大语言模型（LLMs）是一种有前景的替代方案，但由于缺乏标准化的评估方法，其在该领域的有效性尚待探索。为填补这一空白，我们提出了CrypFormBench（简称C.F.B），这是一个同时涵盖符号安全与计算安全的全面基准，用于评估大语言模型的五项核心能力：解释、生成、补全、转换和修正。该基准包含700个实例，覆盖677种方案、7种主流形式化验证器语言和160个安全属性。对9个最先进大语言模型的评估显示，得益于其代码感知优势，大多数模型在解释和补全任务上表现良好，但在生成、转换和修正任务上表现不佳。总体而言，它们的性能仍然有限，其中Claude-3.5取得了最高分48.7分（满分100）。我们进一步提供了实用指南，例如少样本提示、Pass@K采样和轻量级微调，以缓解可执行性瓶颈并提高工具可用的输出。综上所述，我们的基准测试和分析提供了对当前进展的切实见解，并为实现可靠的大语言模型辅助形式化密码分析指明了具体方向。

title: Unlocking Model Potentials Through Adaptive Multi-Agent Scaffolding for Efficient Issue Resolution
authors: Yang Chen, Aliya Ahmad, Yiheng Zhou, Reyhaneh Jabbarvand
abstract: Resolving issues with ambiguous and incomplete descriptions, particularly concerning complex bugs, requires a sophisticated, long-horizon workflow. Agents must navigate codebases to locate the root cause, reproduce the failure, implement a fix, and validate the resulting patch. Inefficient context management, thereby, can lead to rapid context degradation and context poisoning, preventing successful resolution. We propose icat-agent, a decentralized, multi-agent scaffolding that replaces shared context with synchronous, event-based message passing. Utilizing a rubric-based issue quality check, icat-agent strategically pivots its workflow: it initiates parallel patching and validation for well-defined issues, while deploying preliminary exploration for low-quality ones. A comprehensive evaluation of icat-agent on SWE-bench Verified and SWE-bench Pro demonstrates that it consistently outperforms prominent baselines across all difficulty levels, including SWE-agent, mini-SWE-agent, and Claude Code, while using the same underlying models, improving by 3.6-8.4% on SWE-bench Verified and 6.3-18.5% on SWE-bench Pro. icat-agent is also computationally efficient, reducing the average cost by $1.18 per instance compared with the multi-agent Claude Code baseline. Our findings reveal that a robust scaffold such as icat-agent unlocks substantial latent capability within a fixed model, with the same backbone resolving markedly more issues under icat-agent than under existing scaffolds. icat-agent +GPT-5.4-xhigh resolves 67.4% of SWE-bench Pro problems, outperforming the current best result on SWE-bench Pro (59.10%, mini-SWE-agent+GPT-5.4-xhigh) by 8.3 percentage points.
title_zh: 通过自适应多智能体脚手架高效解决问题，释放模型潜力
abstract_zh: 解决模糊和不完整描述（尤其是涉及复杂漏洞）的问题，需要一种复杂且具备长期视野的工作流程。智能体必须遍历代码库找到根本原因、复现故障、实施修复，并验证生成的补丁。因此，低效的上下文管理可能迅速导致上下文质量下降和上下文污染，阻碍问题的成功解决。我们提出 icat-agent，一种去中心化的多智能体脚手架，用基于事件的同步消息传递替代共享上下文。通过基于评分标准的问题质量检测，icat-agent 策略性地调整其工作流程：对于定义清晰的问题，它启动并行的补丁生成与验证；而对于低质量问题，则部署初步探索。在 SWE-bench Verified 和 SWE-bench Pro 上的全面评估表明，icat-agent 在所有难度级别上始终优于包括 SWE-agent、mini-SWE-agent 和 Claude Code 在内的主流基线，且使用相同底层模型，在 SWE-bench Verified 上提升 3.6-8.4%，在 SWE-bench Pro 上提升 6.3-18.5%。icat-agent 还具备计算效率优势，与多智能体 Claude Code 基线相比，每个实例的平均成本降低了 1.18 美元。我们的发现表明，像 icat-agent 这样稳健的支架可以显著释放固定模型中潜在的巨大能力，相同的底层模型在 icat-agent 下解决的问题数量远多于现有支架。icat-agent + GPT-5.4-xhigh 解决了 SWE-bench Pro 中 67.4% 的问题，比当前 SWE-bench Pro 最佳结果（59.10%，mini-SWE-agent + GPT-5.4-xhigh）高出 8.3 个百分点。

title: Representation Matters: An Empirical Study of Program Representations for LLM Vulnerability Reasoning
authors: Andrew Stoltman, Johnathan Tang, Haipeng Cai
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly used for automated vulnerability detection, but it remains unclear how program structure and semantics should be represented for LLM-based reasoning. Most prompting-based approaches provide raw source code, implicitly assuming that more source-level context gives the model better evidence. This paper challenges that assumption through RepBench, an empirical benchmark comparing raw source code with static-analysis-based program representations. RepBench converts real-world C/C++ vulnerability testcases into multiple representations: raw source, Abstract Syntax Trees (ASTs), Control-Flow Graphs (CFGs), Program Dependence Graphs (PDGs), their combinations, and an auxiliary track of enriched PDGs (ePDGs). Using a curated PrimeVul-derived corpus of 107 Joern-based testcases across five CWE categories, we evaluate ten representation variants under a fixed Chain-of-Thought and structured-output protocol, plus 19 additional ePDG cases generated through VulChecker/Hector. Representation choice substantially affects LLM vulnerability reasoning. The strongest variant, AST+PDG, achieves 83.2% accuracy, compared with 53.5% for raw source. At the family level, graph-only prompts outperform both source-only and source-plus-graph prompts while requiring far less prompt overhead. These results reveal a context dilution effect: adding raw source code to compact structural graph evidence can degrade reasoning by making vulnerability-relevant evidence less salient. Overall, our findings show that carefully selected structural representations offer a better accuracy-overhead tradeoff than simply giving LLMs more raw input, and suggest that static analysis can serve as an effective prompt-construction layer for security-focused LLM reasoning.
title_zh: 表示至关重要：一项关于大语言模型漏洞推理中程序表示的实证研究
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLM）越来越多地被用于自动化漏洞检测，但如何表示程序的结构和语义以支持基于LLM的推理仍不明确。大多数基于提示的方法直接提供原始源代码，隐含地假设更多的源代码级上下文能让模型获得更充分的证据。本文通过RepBench基准测试挑战了这一假设，该基准将基于静态分析的程序表示与原始源代码进行实证比较。RepBench将真实世界的C/C++漏洞测试用例转换为多种表示形式：原始源代码、抽象语法树（AST）、控制流图（CFG）、程序依赖图（PDG）及其组合，以及辅助的增强型PDG（ePDG）轨迹。我们利用基于PrimeVul精心整理的语料库（包含107个基于Joern的测试用例，覆盖五个CWE类别），在固定的思维链和结构化输出协议下评估了十种表示变体，另外通过VulChecker/Hector生成了19个额外的ePDG案例。表示方式的选择显著影响LLM的漏洞推理能力。最强变体AST+PDG达到了83.2%的准确率，而原始源代码仅为53.5%。在家族层面上，仅用图的提示优于仅用源代码和源代码加图的提示，同时所需的提示开销更少。这些结果揭示了一种上下文稀释效应：将原始源代码加入紧凑的结构化图证据中，可能会降低漏洞相关证据的显著性，从而削弱推理能力。总体而言，我们的研究结果表明，精心选择的结构化表示比简单地给LLM更多原始输入能提供更好的准确率-开销权衡，并表明静态分析可以成为面向安全LLM推理的有效提示构建层。
